title: Today's AI Brief: As AI gets better, it reveals an empty promise
summary: AI advancements reveal limitations, new GPT capabilities, and the current state of AI
imageAlt: Image from theverge.com for As AI gets better, it reveals an empty promise
imageCaption: As AI gets better, it reveals an empty promise (theverge.com)

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI Brief. As AI gets better, it's starting to show us its limits. I'm looking at a piece from The Verge that says AI can schedule meetings and do tasks, but it can't fix our broken world. That's a pretty sobering thought. I mean, think about it - your AI assistant can help you manage your time, but it can't help you manage the world's problems.

The Verge article argues that AI's capabilities are impressive, but they don't address the deeper issues we face. Your new AI assistant can help with tasks, but it can't solve complex problems like inequality or climate change. That's a bit like having a super-smart calculator that can't help you with your budget. And it's not just about solving problems - it's about understanding the context and nuances of human issues.

On a more positive note, OpenAI has introduced new capabilities to GPT-Rosalind, which advances life sciences research. This includes enhanced biological reasoning, medicinal chemistry expertise, genomics analysis, and experimental workflow capabilities. What's interesting here is how AI is being used to accelerate scientific discovery. For instance, AI can help researchers analyze large datasets and identify patterns that might lead to new treatments or breakthroughs.

As AI gets better, it reveals an empty promise, from theverge.com, is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: AI As AI gets better, it reveals an empty promise Your new assistant can schedule a meeting but it can’t fix our broken world. Your new assistant can schedule a meeting but it can’t fix our broken world. by TC Sottek Jun 3, 2026, 5:45 PM UTC Graphics by Michele Doying / The Verge TC Sottek is a senior editor who has obsessed over headlines and internet spee... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

Introducing new capabilities to GPT-Rosalind is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: GPT-Rosalind advances life sciences research with enhanced biological reasoning, medicinal chemistry expertise, genomics analysis, and experimental workflow capabilities. The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
